description_long: This object consists of a pair of antique-style shotguns, each featuring a curved wooden grip and a dark, metallic barrel. The barrels are relatively short and lack modern features such as ribbing or venting, giving them a classic, flintlock or percussion cap appearance. The wooden grips are smoothly finished and ergonomically shaped, with a warm, natural wood tone. The metalwork is dark and slightly textured, with visible seams and a simple, utilitarian design. There are no visible modern safety mechanisms, and the trigger area is open without a prominent guard. The overall construction suggests these are historical or decorative firearms, likely intended for display or period reenactment.
description: A pair of antique-style shotguns with curved wooden grips and short, dark metal barrels, featuring a simple, classic design.
description_view_0: The shotguns are shown from an angle highlighting the wooden grips and the open trigger areas, with the barrels extending away from the viewer.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the length of the barrels and the smooth curvature of the wooden grips, with both shotguns lying parallel.
description_view_2: The rear of the shotguns is visible, showcasing the rounded ends of the grips and the underside of the barrels.
description_view_3: The shotguns are seen from the side, displaying the full curve of the grips and the simple, unadorned metal barrels.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no ribbing or venting, short barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: absent, open trigger area with no visible guard
